20	Fairness rule:

content.text:
I now have a complete picture of this codebase. It's small and self-contained. Here is my detailed map.

## Codebase Map: `strategic-account-rescue` (mongo lane)

This is an **AST-Bench V2 benchmark target** — a frozen workspace where the workflow implementation is intentionally missing. A coding agent must implement `src/workflow.mjs` so the acceptance test passes. The "before" state fails; the "after" state must satisfy the contract.

### 1. Full directory tree (minus node_modules / .git)

```
.
├── AGENTS.md                                  # Target rules
├── README.md                                  # How to run
├── package.json
├── tests-before.log                           # Captured failing-test output
├── data/
│   └── collections.json                       # Deterministic fixture (the "DB")
├── migrations/
│   └── 001_collection_contract.mongodb.js     # createCollection statements
├── schema/
│   ├── access-paths.md                        # Read/write path contract
│   └── schema.md                              # Collection/field contract + business prompt
├── src/
│   ├── portal-view.mjs                        # Pure projection (read model) — implemented
│   ├── render-proof.mjs                       # SVG/JSON artifact generator
│   └── workflow.mjs                           # ⬅ THE STUB to implement
└── tests/
    └── acceptance.test.mjs                    # The single acceptance test
```

There is **no `node_modules`** and no lockfile — the code uses only Node built-ins (`node:assert`, `node:fs`). It is ESM (`"type": "module"`, `.mjs`).

### 2. Complete `package.json`

```json
{
  "name": "ast-bench-strategic-account-rescue-mongo",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "private": true,
  "type": "module",
  "scripts": {
    "test": "node tests/acceptance.test.mjs",
    "render": "node src/render-proof.mjs"
  }
}
```

No dependencies, no devDependencies. Tests run via plain `node`, no test framework.

### 3. README / docs

- **README.md**: States this is a frozen target; workflow is intentionally missing; acceptance test must fail before implementation. Run `npm test` and `npm run render`.
- **AGENTS.md** (target rules): Implement only Strategic account rescue. Use existing document-shaped collections in `data/`. **Do not change tests. Do not change fixture data.** Run `npm test` before finishing. Keep behavior-equivalent to the acceptance contract.

### 4. Source files and responsibilities

- **`src/workflow.mjs`** (THE STUB — must be implemented): Exports `applyBenchmarkTask(db, now)`. Currently just returns `buildPortalView(db)` with a `// TODO` comment. It must **mutate `db`** (the in-memory fixture object) by persisting workflow state, owner tasks, a customer message, and an audit event, then return the portal view reflecting those writes.

- **`src/portal-view.mjs`** (implemented, read-only projection — do not need to change): Exports `buildPortalView(db)`. Reads `workflow_requests[0]`, finds matching `workflow_state` (by `requestId === request._id`), `owner_tasks`, `audit_events` (with `customerVisible`), and `customer_messages`. Returns a projection object:
  - `taskId`, `title`, `status` (defaults `"Needs work"`), `owner` (task ownerGroups joined by `" + "`, else `"Unassigned"`), `nextStep` (default `"Waiting for workflow"`), `riskSummary` (`"N signals: a, b, …"` or `"Risk not scored"`), `tasks` (`"N owner tasks"`), `history` (`"Audit visible"` / `"Not visible"`), `customerMessage` (or `"No customer-safe message"`).
  - **Key join contract**: portal-view links child records to the request via a field named **`requestId`** that must equal `request._id` (which is `"account-strategic-ac"`). Note the request also has a separate `taskId` field; the link is `requestId`, not `taskId`.

- **`src/render-proof.mjs`** (`npm run render`): Builds before/after views, writes `artifacts/before-after.json` and an `.svg` table. Not involved in the test pass/fail.

### 5. The acceptance test (complete contents) — the escalation/rescue feature spec

`tests/acceptance.test.mjs`:

```javascript
import { deepStrictEqual, strictEqual } from "node:assert";
import { readFileSync } from "node:fs";
import { applyBenchmarkTask } from "../src/workflow.mjs";
import { buildPortalView } from "../src/portal-view.mjs";

const now = "2026-06-18T12:00:00.000Z";
const data = JSON.parse(readFileSync(new URL("../data/collections.json", import.meta.url), "utf8"));
const before = buildPortalView(structuredClone(data));
strictEqual(before.status, "Needs work", "Before state starts without the workflow");
strictEqual(before.history, "Not visible", "Before state has no customer-visible audit trail");
strictEqual(before.tasks, "0 owner tasks", "Before state has no owner tasks");

const runData = structuredClone(data);
const after = applyBenchmarkTask(runData, now);
const expectedStatus = "At-risk escalation active with executive recovery owner routing and customer-visible audit history.";
const expectedOwners = data.workflow_requests[0].ownerGroups;
const expectedSignals = data.workflow_requests[0].riskSignals;

strictEqual(after.status, expectedStatus, "Customer-facing status");
strictEqual(after.owner, expectedOwners.join(" + "), "Owner routing");
strictEqual(after.nextStep.length > 8, true, "Next step exists");
strictEqual(after.history, "Audit visible", "Customer-visible audit trail");
strictEqual(after.customerMessage.length > 12, true, "Customer-safe message");
strictEqual(after.riskSummary, `${expectedSignals.length} signals: ${expectedSignals.map((item) => item.name).join(", ")}`, "Risk summary");
strictEqual(after.tasks, `${expectedOwners.length} owner tasks`, "Owner task count");
assertPersistedState(runData, expectedStatus, expectedOwners, expectedSignals);

console.log("AST-Bench acceptance passed: strategic-account-rescue/mongo");

function assertPersistedState(db, expectedStatus, expectedOwners, expectedSignals) {

  const request = db.workflow_requests[0];
  const state = db.workflow_state.find((item) => item.requestId === request._id);
  strictEqual(Boolean(state), true, "Workflow state must be persisted");
  strictEqual(state.status, expectedStatus, "Persisted status");
  deepStrictEqual(state.riskSignals.map((item) => item.name), expectedSignals.map((item) => item.name), "Persisted risk signals");
  const tasks = db.owner_tasks.filter((item) => item.requestId === request._id);
  strictEqual(tasks.length, expectedOwners.length, "Owner tasks persisted");
  deepStrictEqual(tasks.map((item) => item.ownerGroup), expectedOwners, "Task owner groups");
  strictEqual(tasks.every((item) => item.title && item.dueAt && item.status === "open"), true, "Tasks have production fields");
  strictEqual(db.customer_messages.some((item) => item.requestId === request._id && item.body), true, "Customer message persisted");
  strictEqual(db.audit_events.some((item) => item.requestId === request._id && item.customerVisible), true, "Customer-visible audit event persisted");
}
```

**What the test asserts (the implementation contract for `applyBenchmarkTask`):**

The returned `after` view must have:
- `status` === `"At-risk escalation active with executive recovery owner routing and customer-visible audit history."`
- `owner` === ownerGroups joined by `" + "` → `"Customer Success + Support + Finance + Executive Sponsor"`
- `nextStep` length > 8
- `history` === `"Audit visible"`
- `customerMessage` length > 12
- `riskSummary` === `"6 signals: strategic, account, rescue, enterprise account, open customer impact, audit required"`
- `tasks` === `"4 owner tasks"`

Persisted into `db` (mutate `runData` in place), all child rows keyed by `requestId === request._id` (`"account-strategic-ac"`):
- **`workflow_state`**: exactly one record with `requestId`, `status` (= expectedStatus), and `riskSignals` array whose `.name`s deep-equal the request's signals (order-preserved). Since portal-view also reads `nextStep` from state, the state needs a `nextStep` too (its length is checked via the view; or workflow can set view.nextStep some other way — but the cleanest path is `state.nextStep`).
- **`owner_tasks`**: exactly 4 records (one per ownerGroup, **in order**: Customer Success, Support, Finance, Executive Sponsor), each with `requestId`, `ownerGroup`, and production fields `title` (truthy), `dueAt` (truthy), and `status === "open"`.
- **`customer_messages`**: at least one record with matching `requestId` and a truthy `body` (>12 chars to satisfy the view check).
- **`audit_events`**: at least one record with matching `requestId` and `customerVisible` truthy.

### 6. Data model / schema definitions (verbatim)

**`schema/schema.md`** business prompt and object contract:

> Business prompt: Add an at-risk customer escalation workflow for a delayed high-value order at a strategic account. Combine account tier, contract, support, invoice, usage, shipment, regulatory, and audit context. Route Customer Success, Legal, Finance, and Support. Show a customer-safe portal state and preserve a full audit timeline.
>
> Expected outcome: At-risk escalation active with executive recovery owner routing and customer-visible audit history.

Objects (from schema.md):
- `benchmark_fixture`: 1 doc — `_id, suiteId, taskId, generatedAt`
- `accounts`: 1 doc — `_id, name, tier, region, contract, contacts, context`
- `workflow_requests`: 1 doc — `_id, taskId, accountId, title, primaryEntity, businessPrompt, expectedOutcome, ownerGroups, riskSignals, nextStep, customerMessage`
- `activities`: 6 docs — `_id, accountId, subjectId, summary, occurredAt`
- `workflow_state`: **0 docs** (to be created by the workflow)
- `owner_tasks`: **0 docs** (to be created)
- `audit_events`: **0 docs** (to be created)
- `customer_messages`: **0 docs** (to be created)

**Note on the business prompt vs. fixture mismatch:** The prose prompt says route "Customer Success, Legal, Finance, and Support," but the **fixture's `ownerGroups`** (authoritative for the test) is `["Customer Success", "Support", "Finance", "Executive Sponsor"]` — no "Legal", and includes "Executive Sponsor". The test reads owners from the fixture, so the fixture wins.

**`schema/access-paths.md`** (read/write path):
- Read: load workflow request → reconstruct account context from the 8 collections → resolve owner groups and risk signals → persist state/tasks/message/audit → render portal projection.
- Write: persist one workflow state, all required owner tasks, one customer-safe message, one customer-visible audit event.
- Fairness rule: may stay DB-native but may not change tests or fixture data.

**Migration `migrations/001_collection_contract.mongodb.js`**: just 8 `db.createCollection(...)` calls for the collections above. (This is a `.mongodb.js` shell script — informational; the actual test data source is `data/collections.json`, loaded in-memory.)

The fixture `data/collections.json` is fully quoted in my exploration above. Key values:
- `workflow_requests[0]._id` = `"account-strategic-ac"` (the `requestId` join key)
- `workflow_requests[0].taskId` = `"strategic-account-rescue"`
- `ownerGroups` = `["Customer Success", "Support", "Finance", "Executive Sponsor"]`
- `riskSignals` = 6 objects with `name`/`detail` (names: strategic, account, rescue, enterprise account, open customer impact, audit required)
- `nextStep` = `"Customer Success owner review by 16:00"`
- `customerMessage` = `"Strategic account rescue is being handled by Customer Success, Support, Finance, Executive Sponsor."`
- The 4 write-target collections are empty arrays.

### 7. Database setup (MongoDB)

Despite the "mongo lane" naming and the MongoDB MCP server being connected, **the test does not use a live MongoDB**. The "database" is the JSON object in `data/collections.json`, deep-cloned and mutated in memory. `db` in `applyBenchmarkTask(db, now)` is that plain object; each collection is a property holding an array (or, for `benchmark_fixture`, a single object). The migration file documents the collection contract but isn't executed by the test. No driver, no connection string in code.

### 8. How tests run and what passing looks like

- Run: `npm test` → `node tests/acceptance.test.mjs`. No framework; assertions throw on failure (process exits non-zero).
- **Currently failing** (`tests-before.log`): first failing assertion is "Customer-facing status" — actual `'Needs work'` vs expected `'At-risk escalation active with executive recovery owner routing and customer-visible audit history.'` This is the stub returning the unmodified before-view.
- **Passing** prints exactly: `AST-Bench acceptance passed: strategic-account-rescue/mongo` and exits 0.

**Summary:** This is a self-contained, dependency-free Node ESM benchmark. The only file to implement is `src/workflow.mjs`'s `applyBenchmarkTask(db, now)`, which must mutate the in-memory `db` to persist one `workflow_state` (status + ordered riskSignals + nextStep), four ordered `owner_tasks` (with `title`/`dueAt`/`status:"open"`), one `customer_messages.body`, and one customerVisible `audit_events` — all keyed by `requestId === "account-strategic-ac"` — then return the portal view; the single acceptance test in `tests/acceptance.test.mjs` is the spec, fixture data in `data/collections.json` is authoritative, and neither tests nor fixtures may be modified. I made no changes (read-only exploration).

content.text:
Done. The acceptance test passes.

## What I did

Implemented `applyBenchmarkTask(db, now)` in `src/workflow.mjs` — the only stub in the workspace. The function mutates the in-memory MongoDB-shaped collections to activate the at-risk escalation, then returns the portal projection.

**Persisted state** (all child docs linked to the request via `requestId === "account-strategic-ac"`, the join key `portal-view.mjs` reads):

- **`workflow_state`** — one doc with `status` = the customer-facing escalation status, the request's ordered `riskSignals`, and `nextStep`.
- **`owner_tasks`** — one task per owner group, in escalation order (Customer Success → Support → Finance → Executive Sponsor), each with `title`, `dueAt` (now + 4h SLA), and `status: "open"`.
- **`customer_messages`** — one customer-safe portal message.
- **`audit_events`** — one `customerVisible: true` event preserving the timeline.

**Design choices:**
- All values derive from the fixture request (`ownerGroups`, `riskSignals`, `expectedOutcome`, `customerMessage`) rather than hard-coded strings — no simulated evidence.
- Documents stay native document-shaped (nested arrays/objects, `_id`-keyed, joined by `requestId`).
- State/tasks/message writes are idempotent (filter-then-push) so a re-run supersedes cleanly.

**Constraints honored:** only `src/workflow.mjs` changed; tests and fixture data untouched.

**Result:** `npm test` → exit 0, prints `AST-Bench acceptance passed: strategic-account-rescue/mongo`.
